summary: PR 2472 added a new C implementation for src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94.c centered on hsd_803A949C and exposed its prototype in hsd_3A94.h. The function handles command entries for CARD-file-related state, close/retry behavior, checksum/verification calls, icon-size-dependent offsets, memcpy/memcmp transfers, and command queue advancement. The only textual feedback present is an automated decomp report showing hsd_803A949C improved from 0.00% to 58.78%; no PR body or human review comments were supplied.

key_files.role: Added the hsd_803A949C implementation plus local helper functions and temporary local structs for card command state and block layout.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_3A94.c added includes for hsd_3AA7.h, hsd_3B2B.h, hsd_3B2E.h, dolphin/card.h, string.h
raw_diff: hsd_3A94.c defines CardStateExt, CardCmdEntry, CardBlock, hsd_803A949C_GetEntry, hsd_803A949C_Close, hsd_803A949C_IconSize, hsd_803A949C_FileId, and hsd_803A949C

key_files.role: Added the public prototype for hsd_803A949C.

what_changed.text: Implemented hsd_803A949C as a large switch over entry->x0 command values, with cases 1, 2, 5, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 13, 15, and 16 visible in the diff.
Added local layout structs CardStateExt, CardCmdEntry, and CardBlock to express offsets used by the new function.
Factored repeated operations into static helpers for command entry lookup, close-with-retry, icon size selection, and file ID extraction.
Used CARD and string APIs, including CARDClose, memcpy, and memcmp, plus calls to hsd_803B31CC, hsd_803B2B20, and hsd_803AC558.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: CARDClose, memcpy, memcmp, hsd_803B31CC, hsd_803B2B20, hsd_803AC558

what_changed.text: At function end, cleared the command entry state, reset hsd_804D799C, and advanced hsd_804D7980 modulo 128.

smart_moves.text: Introduced temporary structs with offset comments instead of leaving the implementation entirely as raw byte offsets.
Extracted small repeated expressions into helpers: close retry loop, icon size switch, file ID composition, and current command entry lookup.
Kept the recovered function signature in the owning header rather than only defining the body in the C file.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_3A94.h added void hsd_803A949C(s32 arg1);

decomp_lessons.lesson: For large callback/state-machine functions, a command switch plus small local helpers can make a partial match reviewable without requiring every field name to be semantically recovered first.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: helper functions hsd_803A949C_Close, hsd_803A949C_IconSize, hsd_803A949C_FileId

decomp_lessons.lesson: Offset-documented temporary structs are useful bridge code when recovering a binary data layout; this PR used them for CARD state, command entries, and card block contents.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: typedef struct CardStateExt with offset comments
raw_diff: typedef struct CardCmdEntry with offset comments
raw_diff: typedef struct CardBlock with offset comments

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function body is added, add the corresponding prototype to the owning header if the symbol is externally visible.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_3A94.h added /* 3A949C */ void hsd_803A949C(s32 arg1);

naming_conventions.text: New local helper names were prefixed with the owning symbol address-style name hsd_803A949C_, e.g. hsd_803A949C_Close, hsd_803A949C_IconSize, hsd_803A949C_FileId.
Struct fields remained mostly offset-style names such as x0, x8, x3B0, and x430, indicating semantics were not fully recovered in this slice.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: CardStateExt fields x0, x8, x3B0, x430
raw_diff: CardCmdEntry fields x0, x8, xC, x10, x14, x18, x20

naming_conventions.text: Local variables used role names for recovered purposes such as entry, state, block, result, icon_size, offset, remaining, data_offset, hdr_plus_icon, slot, and sector_size.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used a do-while retry loop for CARDClose until the result is not -1 or ten attempts have occurred.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_803A949C_Close do { result = CARDClose(&state->xC); ... i++; } while (i < 10);

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used a switch for icon-size selection from state->x3B0, returning 0x1800 for case 2, 0xE00 for case 1, and 0 otherwise.
Recovered file ID composition as a high-byte/low-byte expression, (block->x10 << 8) | block->x11.
Used temporary typed structs and casts for card data blocks and state buffers rather than expressing every access as M2C_FIELD.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: field accesses such as block->x12 and state->x430

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use helper extraction to make large command callbacks reviewable
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2472, hsd_803A949C was recovered as a large command switch, while repeated or meaningful operations were split into local helpers for entry lookup, close retry, icon size, and file ID extraction. This is useful evidence for a local pattern: split small repeated operations out of large state-machine callbacks when it improves reviewability and still follows the observed control flow.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefix static helpers with owning address-style function name when semantics are local
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2472 used helper names hsd_803A949C_Close, hsd_803A949C_IconSize, and hsd_803A949C_FileId for static helpers local to hsd_803A949C. This is a candidate local naming pattern, not a global rule.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static helper declarations named hsd_803A949C_*

source_update_candidates.title: hsd_3A94.c contains CARD/memory-card command callback work
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2472 added hsd_803A949C in src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94.c. The implementation includes dolphin/card.h, uses CARDFileInfo and CARDClose, and processes command entries with checksum/compare/copy behavior around card-state buffers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: memcpy/memcmp and hsd_803B31CC/hsd_803B2B20 calls in hsd_803A949C
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact for search/navigation; semantics should be curator-verified before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not record this PR as a full match: the only loaded validation evidence is an automated report showing 58.78% after, not 100%.
Do not infer exact semantic names for CardStateExt/CardCmdEntry/CardBlock fields; the diff mostly uses offset-style xNN names.
Do not promote the static helper prefix pattern as a global naming standard from this single PR.
Do not claim human reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments were loaded.
